# ggscribe

The goal of ggscribe is to …

## Installation

You can install the development version of ggscribe from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("davidhodge931/ggscribe")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

``` r
library(ggscribe)
## basic example code
```

What is special about using `README.Rmd` instead of just `README.md`?
You can include R chunks like so:

``` r
summary(cars)
#>      speed           dist       
#>  Min.   : 4.0   Min.   :  2.00  
#>  1st Qu.:12.0   1st Qu.: 26.00  
#>  Median :15.0   Median : 36.00  
#>  Mean   :15.4   Mean   : 42.98  
#>  3rd Qu.:19.0   3rd Qu.: 56.00  
#>  Max.   :25.0   Max.   :120.00
```

You’ll still need to render `README.Rmd` regularly, to keep `README.md`
up-to-date. `devtools::build_readme()` is handy for this.

You can also embed plots, for example:

![](reference/figures/README-pressure-1.png)

In that case, don’t forget to commit and push the resulting figure
files, so they display on GitHub and CRAN.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`scribe_axis_line()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_axis_line.md)
  : Annotate axis line segment
- [`scribe_axis_text()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_axis_text.md)
  : Annotate axis text
- [`scribe_axis_ticks()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_axis_ticks.md)
  : Annotate axis ticks segments
- [`scribe_curve()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_curve.md)
  : Annotate curve
- [`scribe_panel_grid()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_panel_grid.md)
  : Annotate panel grid segments
- [`scribe_panel_shade()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_panel_shade.md)
  : Annotate the panel background
- [`scribe_segment()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_segment.md)
  : Annotate segment
- [`scribe_text()`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggscribe/reference/scribe_text.md)
  : Annotate text
